title: Bulk gDNA Extraction from Coral Samples
author: Andrew Baker
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.dyq7vv

steps.title: Acquire Sample Material
steps.instruction: Acquire sample material. Small amounts of coral tissue, with or without skeleton, may be obtained by clipping off branch tips, scraping with a razor blade, etc. Greater success in downstream PCR amplification is generally achieved when the amount of starting material is small. Tissue scrapings or biopsies are ideal for the in situ digests described here using SDS and Proteinase K. Larger core samples or colony fragments are not recommended - in this case blast the tissue off the skeleton first using an airbrush or Water Pik and then centrifuge the blastate and lyse cells in 1% SDS and digest with Proteinase K.

steps.title: Lyse Sample with SDS
steps.instruction: Add sample to 500 µL 1% SDS in DNAB in a microcentrifuge tube. Be sure that sample is fully immersed in the buffer. Incubate sample for 60-90 minutes at 65°C. Sample is now stabilized for storage at room temperature, and can be treated as an "archive" for future use. These archives can be used for multiple attempts at DNA extraction.

steps.title: Proteinase K Digestion
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL Proteinase K (10 mg/mL) to sample archive and vortex well. Incubate overnight at 37°C, for 6-7 hours at 45°C, or for 2-3 hours at 55°C.

steps.title: Prepare Aliquots
steps.instruction: Prepare a new set of 1.5 mL tubes for the samples you intend to process, and add 100 µL of each sample archive (in 1% SDS in DNAB) to the new set of tubes. Return the remainder of the sample archive to storage. DNA can be extracted from any quantity of sample archive. The protocol given here is written for extractions of 100 µL of the sample archive, but volumes can be proportionally adjusted up to the second ethanol precipitation if desired (e.g., to increase absolute DNA yields).

steps.title: Add CTAB Mix
steps.instruction: Defrost CTAB mix (stored at -20°C) and add twice volume (200 µL) to each sample. Vortex and incubate at 65°C for 30-60 minutes.

steps.title: Chloroform Extraction
steps.instruction: Allow samples to cool. In fume hood, add equal volume (300 µL) of chloroform. Be sure to ‘charge’ (i.e., fill and empty pipette tip with chloroform 2 to 3 times) the pipette tip before first use, or your tip will leak chloroform. Vortex sample and invert several times, but be careful that caps are tight – leaking chloroform will erase your sample labels! Put in rack on rotating platform for 2-3 hours.

steps.title: Centrifuge and Transfer Supernatant
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000g (RCF) for 10 minutes. Align tubes in centrifuge so that hinges are on the outside. While spinning, prepare a new set of labeled 1.5 mL tubes. Remove samples from centrifuge and very carefully pipette off top ~250 µL into new tube. Dispose the rest of the contents into appropriate waste container.

steps.title: Ethanol Precipitation (First)
steps.instruction: Add twice volume (500 µL) of 100% (200-proof) ethanol (EtOH). Ensure caps are shut tightly and invert samples in their rack several times, together with a few brief shakes to make sure samples are well mixed.

steps.title: Incubate for DNA Precipitation
steps.instruction: Put samples in freezer for at least 2 hours to promote DNA precipitation. If the EtOH is pre-chilled, you can leave it in the -20°C freezer for only a 1/2 hour. Potential stopping point. Keep samples in freezer to allow DNA to precipitate.

steps.title: Centrifuge DNA Pellet
steps.instruction: Put samples in centrifuge (ensuring that the hinges of the tubes are on the outside) and spin for 10 minutes at 10,000g (RCF).

steps.title: Decant Ethanol
steps.instruction: Remove samples from centrifuge and carefully decant off ethanol from all the tubes into a waste container. The DNA pellet should remain stuck to the inside of the tube.

steps.title: Dry DNA Pellet (First)
steps.instruction: Put tubes, with their caps open, in the Vacufuge/Speedvac. Be careful when putting the tubes in and don’t touch the inside of the caps. Speedvac at 45°C for 30-60 minutes.

steps.title: Resuspend Pellet in NaOAc
steps.instruction: Remove samples from vacufuge and add 100 µL of 0.3 M NaOAc (do not use the stock 3 M solution!). Vortex sample well to dissolve pellet. When the pellet is dissolved the sample will appear “syrupy” and will not bounce around as droplets inside the tube.

steps.title: Ethanol Precipitation (Second)
steps.instruction: Once the pellet is dissolved, add 200uL of 100% Ethanol, vortex and invert several times and put in freezer for at least 2hrs. Potential stopping point. Keep sample in freezer to allow DNA to precipitate.

steps.title: Centrifuge and Decant Supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove samples from freezer, and centrifuge for 10 minutes at 10,000g (RCF). Decant supernatant into appropriate waste container.

steps.title: Ethanol Wash
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of 70% Ethanol, and vortex thoroughly (this is the “Ethanol Wash” step). Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 10,000g (RCF), and again decant supernatant into appropriate waste container.

steps.title: Dry DNA Pellet (Second)
steps.instruction: Put samples in Vacufuge with the caps open, and speedvac at 45°C for 30-60 minutes to thoroughly dry the pellet.

steps.title: Resuspend in TE Buffer
steps.instruction: Take samples out of centrifuge and add 50-100 µL TE buffer. Vortex briefly to mix and store at -20°C in freezer. Sample is now ready for PCR. Store DNA samples at -20°C.
